mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 5 Series 535i engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 535i engine typically costs £900–£12,000 supply only depending on generation. M30-era engines sit at the lower end (£900–£2,800), while B58 units run £4,000–£12,000. Fitting adds £650–£1,500. Reconditioned N55 engines start from around £2,200. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW 5 Series 535i engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends entirely on which of the eight engine generations your 535i has. The M30 (E28/E34) is a classic, robust six. The M62 V8 (E39) is smooth but timing chain guides are a concern. The N54 (E60 2006–2010) has HPFP and injector issues. The N55 (F10) is a significant improvement and more reliable than the N54. The B58 (G30 2017+) is widely regarded as one of BMW's best modern engines. Check the Era Map above for your specific car.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 5 Series 535i?

acceptedAnswer.text: HPFP and injector failure on the N54 (E60 2006–2010), timing chain guide failure on the M62 (E39), oil leaks from valve cover and oil filter housing gaskets on the N55 (F10), and cooling system vulnerabilities on the B58 (G30). The N54's HPFP is the most expensive common failure.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 5 Series 535i engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and generation. Against a well-kept £6,500 F10 535i, a £4,500 recon is 69% of the car's value — borderline. Against a tired £4,500 example, the same recon exceeds our 70% BMW threshold — get a scrap valuation first. For G30 B58-engined cars (2017+), higher vehicle values (£16,500–£45,000) make replacement more viable.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 5 Series 535i engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. As a rule of thumb: E28 (1985–1988) is M30B34, E34 (1988–1991) is M30B35, E34 (1991–1996) is M60B30/M62B35, E39 (1996–2003) is M62B35/M62TUB35, E60 (2006–2010) is N54B30A, F10 (2010–2016) is N55B30, G30 (2017–2023) is B58B30. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the N54 HPFP, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: The HPFP typically requires replacement rather than repair (£500–£1,500). BMW extended warranty coverage for HPFPs on certain N54-equipped vehicles. Injectors often fail around the same time — when one injector fails, the others are often not far behind. Replacement requires proper coding by a specialist or BMW dealer.

eraMap.title: The 535i THROUGH TIME — Era Map

rows.eraNote: Classic SOHC six — robust but ageing; fuel injection system
Later M30 — 211 hp, reliable but parts becoming scarce
Early V8 — timing chain guides, Vanos, cooling system
Solid V8 — timing chain guides, valley pan, valve stem seals
VANOS V8 — timing guides, Vanos seals, cooling system
Single-turbo six — improved but gaskets leak
Modern B58 — robust, cooling system, oil leaks

eraMap.sourceNote: This table is the single source every other section on this page matches — Sections 2, 4 and 6 reference it rather than restating conflicting figures.

rows.bestFor: Insurance jobs, warranty claims (modern cars only)

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the current B58 generation, the most common quote request today. Older generations: see the Era Map above for M30, M60, M62, N54, and N55-specific pricing, which runs significantly lower. Reconditioned N55 engines start from around £2,200. Used engine fitted costs typically range from £2,450 upwards.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a 5 Series 535i engine typically takes 10–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent). V8 engines (E34/E39) and N54/N55 turbo engines require more time than the M30 six-cylinder.

cta.label: → Compare 5 Series 535i engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: N54 High-Pressure Fuel Pump & Injector Failure (E60 2006–2010)
problems.symptoms: Long cranking, rough idle, loss of power, engine warning light, stalling, misfires.
problems.repairCost: £500–£1,500 (HPFP replacement); £1,200+ for a full set of injectors.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only; ignoring symptoms can lead to further engine damage.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if symptoms appear.

problems.recommendation: The N54's HPFP is its most infamous weak point. BMW faced a class-action lawsuit over defective HPFPs and turbos. The original Bosch pumps had design flaws, with most failures occurring between 60,000–160,000 km. Fuel injectors often fail around the same time — when one goes, the others are often not far behind. Replacement requires proper coding by a specialist or BMW dealer. Use high-octane fuel and avoid thrashing the car from cold.

problems.title: M62 Timing Chain Guide Failure (E39 1996–2003)
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise from the engine on cold start, metallic ticking, VANOS fault codes, engine warning light.
problems.typicalMileage: 80,000–150,000 miles — plastic guides become brittle with age.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only if caught early; catastrophic failure requires full engine replacement.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle, book an inspection today.

problems.recommendation: The M62 V8's timing chain guides are made of plastic that deteriorates with age. When they break, pieces drop into the oil pan and the chain can slip, leading to piston-valve contact in this interference engine. Valve stem seals can also fail, causing blue smoke on startup. If you hear any rattling from the engine, especially on cold start, book an inspection immediately.

problems.title: Oil Leaks (Valve Cover & Oil Filter Housing Gaskets — N55 F10)
problems.symptoms: Burning oil smell, visible oil on the engine or garage floor, low oil level warnings.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only; if ignored, oil leaks can lead to oil starvation.

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon if symptoms appear.

problems.recommendation: The N55 is prone to oil leaks from the valve cover gasket and oil filter housing gasket. Oil dripping from the oil filter housing gasket can contaminate the serpentine belt, leading to belt failure and potential engine damage. The valve cover itself can also crack. If you notice a burning oil smell or visible oil, have the gaskets inspected promptly.

rows.whenItMakesSense: Essential repair — vehicle may be undriveable without it
Preventative — catches fault before catastrophic failure
Common maintenance item — repair when symptoms appear
Address blue smoke on startup — specialist job
Full engine replacement required if bottom-end or valvetrain fails

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 535i? Check These First
buyIf: * E28/E34 M30 with full service history — classic, robust six
* E39 M62 with timing chain guides recently replaced — solid V8 with proper maintenance
* F10 N55 with gaskets replaced and service history — reliable turbo six
* G30 B58 with full service history — one of BMW's best modern engines
avoidIf: * 2006–2010 N54 with no HPFP or injector history — expensive repairs await
* E39 M62 with timing chain rattle — engine-out job
* Poor service history on any generation — these engines are unforgiving of neglect
* Overheating or coolant loss on any generation — can indicate head gasket failure

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 535i badge spans a remarkable range of engines — from the bulletproof M30 six to the twin-turbo N54, the improved N55, and the modern B58. The M30 and M62 V8s are classic choices but need timing chain vigilance. The N54 is best avoided unless you have a repair fund. The N55 is a reliable sweet spot. The B58 (2017+) is arguably BMW's best modern straight-six. With the 70% BMW scrap threshold, a £4,500 recon on a £6,500 F10 535i is 69% — borderline. On a sub-£5,000 example, think carefully.

cta.label: → Compare 5 Series 535i engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 5 Series 535i (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling on startup (M62 timing chain / N54 wastegate)
* Service History — Including which engine era and confirmed oil-interval discipline
* HPFP/Injector History — Any replacement on N54? Keep £1,200+ in reserve
* Oil Leaks — Check around valve cover and oil filter housing (N55 common)
* Timing Chain Documentation — Any evidence of chain guide replacement on M62?
* Smoke Check — Blue smoke on startup could indicate valve stem seals (M62)
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
